New York City saw major civic and policy activity this week, from a large Staten Island commercial fire and mass anti-Trump protests to pushes on affordable housing, transit access and free buses, climate and parks funding, and city legal fights over ride-hailing and workplace discrimination.

### Risks & Alerts

- Fire on a Staten Island commercial block prompted a large FDNY response and an emergency alert for nearby residents. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)

### Civics & Politics

- NYC sued to shut down unlicensed ride-hail app Empower, which could affect riders seeking cheaper alternatives. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMipgFBVV95cUxPY1EtMjFTNzl0V0UyRk85OUlwWWI0X19OaDJsRnBDS3NGM09meXU4V1Vrb0F0ZnJQZmxSVEhYajJPaHZHVncwU04zMHVrdFB2WHN1NUVrQzJDT1dtSkYwMXRlV090Zy15dTdkMEdvVVBFM2tXV0FfYXJ6QUJMNXZ5U00yTFFQQk0yWmUxR1hLTlZseEFXZ0lEbk9EZzN5R3ZyV25GN1RR?oc=5)
- Mayor Mamdani moved to speed affordable housing development across NYC, potentially affecting neighborhood growth and housing availability. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- Lawmakers urged Gov. Hochul to revive NYC’s free bus pilot, a proposal that could lower commuting costs. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMilAFBVV95cUxQdWpnRy1ISFNWVDhXckc3NTVrZldJVkVKSFAxbmVoMVotNTFyNFFuR0l6QkJuWTBLaHc3T2RrTXl0cDNkN09nN1Z4NzBvNVNfYWRTbVdMZ2RDV3hZMERuRkRfNjdPQXgxSmFuaWh0cE4xc1ZRWUpZOEFpY2J1d3ZlZlhDd0tPaFAwYllPUzhNZjk1U0JN?oc=5)
- The mayor’s rent guidelines board held its first meeting, starting a process that can affect stabilized rents citywide. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- Youth advocates entered Albany’s climate law budget debate as the state budget deadline approached. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiqgFBVV95cUxPbmFKbTlPZVFUeTFBNkwxZkFkaUJTUHpRVEE2Z3VtNE5oS3doTC1VU0ROMWRaSjNsT1BBS2NEZlowS1dLMjRjNGdXMEdxWjU3TkdSY3FSd3g4STNLZEZRQ0FVcVFmaTlzalJXMWZuTUZJc2dhVnljejhnWWRlUk44bnlJWXlPRkdBWmxoTHVvcFFKQm55U1VsTmpGa3BvVGYwdlpWeWNRbE4wdw?oc=5)
- Parks advocates rallied for dedicating 1% of the city budget to NYC Parks funding. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMihgFBVV95cUxQQWdrMTEyU0ZQdm14bVJRbjA5OVA1QndONzNZdGQ1V0o0WlN6Tk9laHZSZ2J4aUNzZFZONDM2YUwzVVZHYzRmMDhIV2hGTVMwaFZSWkh6OUpFaWJxYXZ2NWhwaDc1VXRDcjdlZzdrLThWd2Q3akdreGVWanViSE9kMDRhOHl2QQ?oc=5)

### Opportunities & Welfare

- Travelers should prepare for possible ICE encounters at NYC-area airports and review their rights before flying. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMipAFBVV95cUxOclVrSFpfaWt0c19pOHlleVZlUi05N1A2cGMtRFVrb3ZGQnBrY0VSQzhFYi1hV09qT09hWFpsUkNfb0ZIb1NWUWw3SURlOWxpUjVNaExsdTlRd0UzWGtKQ2kxMXRMRjRZTHVnQ0RMZDZJSnhJZUJsbTZxVGJtclhWT2tPbEFDM1NEWkhZVVN4TEFhQzlaZmwxRGh0YmZWajJ3c1gwMg?oc=5)
- MTA plans more training so express bus drivers can better operate wheelchair lifts for riders with disabilities. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMihgFBVV95cUxPNHNQRVlPY0VFR09xLXZrbHdCbE1FMkV4WlJMclhsaUFteVRub1BKLTQ4cEFhUVVvNjgyb2V2TE9qcmlId0ItOGkzZGNHdnVTOVVlN3RuNk5CekFNRWFhQmJudkxPa3ZYYkM5Ym5zcUVwMmhLQ3Nzb0I4WlBPanZjM0NGeUoxUQ?oc=5)

### Community

- Major construction will shut down a Staten Island street through June, so drivers and bus riders should plan alternate routes. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- Smorgasburg, Queens Night Market, and JAPAN Fes are returning in 2026, offering seasonal dining and outing options citywide. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiiAFBVV95cUxQX3IxQ1RkckoybzRTM1lVbzBxRkJGbGJfUFVFdk9LS3FsRGdxX2w2ZTZoRWk4ZllndzFIRzVXQlAyMV9EQlhUTU9Ec2FybExPQ1lmSDE4X2tmVmhGU1dVeEl3WUJscjJNRHpsOHVRUHRZSXlnaTMzMzBMcWRHdmNWNmpvRnFheHZM?oc=5)
- Recent NYC restaurant health inspection scores are available, which may affect where residents choose to dine. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
